text: Our recent paper on “Flexible High-Resolution 3D Image Synthesis Via Global Image Structure Modeling with VQ-GAN” was just accepted to MICCAI. This paper presents a flexible high-resolution 3D image synthesis framework that can generate diverse 3D medical images with robustness to arbitrary spatial transformations such as rotation and translation. The proposed framework can achieve efficient and fast 3D image synthesis while utilizing the latent space of VQ-GAN to speed up computations. Our proposed method can be extensively validated on a wide range of 3D medical image datasets in different anatomical locations, as our method can adapt to different datasets with only a few examples, shown as four 3D medical imaging datasets with small number of examples (n ≤ 1844), and it achieves better performance than several state-of-the-art GANs with higher diversity and less mode collapse. Moreover, due to the limitations of evaluation metrics for medical images, we have evaluated the quality of our generated images using clinical insights from radiologists, confirming the high clinical fidelity of the synthesized data. In addition, in the case of low resource settings, we demonstrated that our synthetic images can boost the performance of a state-of-the-art segmentation model, Swin-UNETR, trained in our collaborating institutions, which could benefit resource-limited hospitals in medical image analysis.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
